subject: Adding libraries for Selenium tests
commit_message:
Adding libraries for Selenium tests

Adding missing libraries to be able to use selenium headless
Still need apt-get install xvfb to work

Change-Id: Idcd900bdf7de34d29b02f8caf50dc152067c86a9
Signed-off-by: eh552t <eh552t@intl.att.com>
Issue-Id: CLAMP-28

Thank you. 

I just restarted the job on the sandbox: https://jenkins.onap.org/sandbox/job/clamp-master-csit-UIs/6/console and still have the same issue: EnvironmentError: Can not find Xvfb. Please install it and try again. 

That's the same issue i get when removing xvfb (installed with apt-get) on my local environment (missing the file /usr/bin/xvfb-run )

Patch Set 1:

Hi Jeremy,
In the build https://jenkins.onap.org/sandbox/job/clamp-master-csit-UIs/8/consoleFull node used is  snd-ubuntu1604-docker-8c-8g-7953 
Is it possible to know if xvfb-run is well present on the node? It seems this is the key part installed with the command apt-get-update xvfb

Patch Set 1:

hi Eddy, yes.. I replied in the ticket that I have upgraded the sandbox and prod machines... let me check on your question now..

Patch Set 1:

Hi Eddy, you can now use the docker machine too which has xvfb
I ran a job for you: https://jenkins.onap.org/sandbox/job/clamp-master-csit-UIs/11/

Patch Set 1:

> Hi Eddy, you can now use the docker machine too which has xvfb
 > I ran a job for you: https://jenkins.onap.org/sandbox/job/clamp-master-csit-UIs/11/

Thank you all, xvfb is well ok now :-)

But forgot that to make it work i had also to have geckodriver executable in the PATH because next error is: WebDriverException: Message: 'geckodriver' executable needs to be in PATH.

File can be downloaded from here: https://github.com/mozilla/geckodriver/releases/download/v0.18.0/geckodriver-v0.18.0-linux64.tar.gz and uncompressed somewhere available in the PATH. What would be the best way?
Thank you,
Eddy

Patch Set 1:

And it seems Firefox needs to be installed as well even if working in headless mode. I tried to uninstall firefox from my local environment and got the same error than on the sandbox after i installed geckodriver manually as a workaround.

Patch Set 1:

hmmm Let me get back to you on that one.. I have not found a case like this yet..
